stg_missing_criteria: Minimum doctor qualification not specified in the clinical presentation
validated.stg_reasoning: The patient satisfies multiple STG indications. First, the patient has a well-documented history of 8-9 disabling episodes of tonsillitis per year for the past three years, associated with high fever, severe throat pain, and difficulty swallowing. This frequency exceeds all specified sub-criteria for recurrent tonsillitis (>=7 in the preceding year, >=5 in each of the preceding two years, and >=3 in each of the three preceding years). Second, the patient meets the indication for tonsillar and adenoid hyperplasia, presenting with Grade III bilateral tonsillar enlargement and an enlarged adenoid shadow (adenoid-nasopharynx ratio 0.7), which results in habitual loud snoring, mouth breathing (suggestive of obstructive sleep apnea), and difficulty swallowing. All three mandatory frequency thresholds for recurrent tonsillitis are also met based on the reported 8-9 episodes per year for the past three years. The clinical diagnosis is well-supported by the patient's symptoms and examination findings.
